title: Operating-System Landscape, Filesystems, Editions, and Lifecycle
description: An organization supports Windows, macOS, Linux, ChromeOS, iOS, Android, appliances, and embedded devices with incompatible applications, filesystems, update windows, and support dates. Learners cumulatively produce operating-system selection and lifecycle register through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish desktop, mobile, server, hypervisor, appliance, embedded, real-time, cloud-managed, open-source, and proprietary operating-system responsibilities.
Compare common filesystem identity, case behavior, permissions, encryption, compression, journaling, snapshots, quotas, compatibility, and repair boundaries.
Map edition, architecture, hardware, management, security, virtualization, directory, remote-access, language, and licensing capabilities to requirements.
